document_type: HUF Domain Accuracy Quadruple — Real-Data Instantiation
design_principle: No data goes untested. All four a_i formulations are computed for every domain, every snapshot. Overengineering is a problem of efficiency; with sufficient compute, discarding metrics costs information while computing them costs nothing. The amplifier sees one primary a_i; the observation layer retains all four.

a0_internal_balance.measures: How evenly a domain distributes across its own carriers
a0_internal_balance.valid_for: Temporal drift within a fixed domain (same n over time)
a0_internal_balance.invalid_for: Cross-domain comparison when carrier counts differ (Keff_fill paradox)

a1_fleet_alignment.reference: Global fleet distribution (same for all domains)
a1_fleet_alignment.measures: How much each domain deviates from the system-wide carrier mix
a1_fleet_alignment.caveat: Conflates typicality with diversity — a concentrated domain matching the fleet average scores high
a1_fleet_alignment.formal_alignment: Closest to accepted SA rework definition: a_i = 1 - TV(predicted, realized)

a2_concentration_resistance.measures: What fraction of the domain is NOT dominated by its single largest carrier
a2_concentration_resistance.valid_for: Cross-domain diversity ranking. Only metric that produces correct operational ranking.
a2_concentration_resistance.note: Simplest metric, most interpretable, hardest to game. Does not use TV distance.

a3_carrier_completeness.reference: Uniform over all n_global models (1/n_global each), domain zero-padded to global model set
a3_carrier_completeness.measures: How much of the full system's carrier catalog this domain participates in
a3_carrier_completeness.valid_for: Structural isolation detection — a domain with few carriers relative to the system
a3_carrier_completeness.caveat: Compressed range in practice. Punishes domains for not carrying models they may never need.

structural_interpretation.a0_high_a1_high: Domain is internally balanced AND typical of fleet → healthy, stable domain
structural_interpretation.a0_high_a1_low: Domain is internally balanced BUT atypical → specialized domain, different model mix from fleet
structural_interpretation.a0_low_a1_high: Domain is internally unbalanced BUT typical → concentrated on popular models (common pattern)
structural_interpretation.a0_low_a1_low: Domain is internally unbalanced AND atypical → structurally isolated, potential risk
structural_interpretation.a2_low: Single-carrier dominance regardless of other metrics → monoculture risk
structural_interpretation.a3_low: Carrier catalog gap regardless of other metrics → structural isolation from system

amplifier_wiring.primary_a_i: Design choice — not a theorem. Options: a1 (formal TV-based, matches SA rework definition) or a2 (correct operational ranking, simplest). Both are defensible.
amplifier_wiring.observation_layer: All four metrics computed and stored for every domain at every snapshot.
diagnostic_flags: If a1 is high but a2 is low: domain is typical but concentrated — red flag
If a0 drops over time while a1 stays stable: domain is drifting internally without diverging from fleet — early warning
If a3 drops: domain is losing carrier types — structural isolation in progress
amplifier_wiring.amplification_function: f operates on the primary a_i. The other three are CL-03 observation-layer diagnostics that inform the operator, not the controller.

pll_compliance.design_choices_labeled: Primary a_i selection is explicitly a design choice, not derived from physics
pll_compliance.no_free_parameters: All four metrics are parameter-free given the carrier distribution

ranking_validation.operational_ground_truth: sac0 (most diverse, 66 models, 13.1% max) > phx1 ≈ sac2 (moderate) > ams5 (concentrated, 32.4% max) > iad1 (monoculture, 76.5% max)
ranking_validation.a0_ranking: iad1 > ams5 > phx1 > sac0 > sac2 — INVERTED (normalization paradox)
ranking_validation.a1_ranking: phx1 > sac0 > sac2 > iad1 > ams5 — partially correct, conflates typicality
ranking_validation.a2_ranking: sac0 > phx1 > sac2 > ams5 > iad1 — CORRECT, matches operational reality
ranking_validation.a3_ranking: sac0 > sac2 > phx1 > ams5 > iad1 — correct direction, compressed range
